items.title: US-Iran talks postponed as Vance pulls out of Switzerland trip
items.summary: The talks were expected to focus on implementing the deal to end the war signed earlier this week.

items.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes kill 18 as Israel says four soldiers killed by Hezbollah
items.summary: It comes a day after the US and Iran signed a deal to end their conflict, including fighting in Lebanon.

items.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
items.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

items.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
items.summary: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

items.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
items.summary: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
items.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.

items.title: Asia's richest man Ambani announces what could be India's biggest share sale
items.summary: India's largest telecom operator is expected to raise around $4bn (£3.02bn), according to media reports.

items.title: In Trump's shadow, Vance becomes face of Iran deal
items.summary: His fierce defence of the Iran plan amid mounting criticism comes as speculation intensifies about a possible 2028 presidential run.

items.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
items.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

items.title: Parents of Serbia's teenage school shooter given jail terms in retrial
items.summary: The boy was 13 when he killed eight girls, a boy and a school guard at Vladislav Ribnikar school in Belgrade in 2023.

items.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
items.summary: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.

items.title: British man dies in paragliding accident in Spain
items.summary: Authorities in Catalonia confirmed that the 63-year-old died in the Palau de Noguera area on Wednesday.

items.title: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
items.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.

items.title: Jury fails to reach verdict in Norwegian 'hitman' trial
items.summary: Johannes Natland, 19, was remanded in custody and will now face a retrial.

items.title: Obama moved to tears by wife Michelle's speech
items.summary: The couple were opening a site in Chicago, which the former president described as a "celebration of community".

items.title: Bird flu kills more than 75% of baby seals on remote Australian island, study finds
items.summary: Scientists say 13,000 southern elephant seal pups on Heard Island have died of the H5N1 strain.

items.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
items.summary: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.

items.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
items.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

items.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
items.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

items.title: Teenager dies in horse-drawn carriage accident in New York
items.summary: The horse bolted and toppled over after the carriage driver stepped down to take a picture of the passengers.

items.title: Lil Nas X says he's 'feeling better' after rehab and bipolar diagnosis
items.summary: The Old Town Road star was arrested last year for attacking police officers and later entered rehab.

items.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
items.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

items.title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
items.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

items.title: Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life
items.summary: In fiery exchange, judge sentenced him to three life sentences plus 25 years to life on four other charges, all running consecutively.

items.title: The Ring and Lilo & Stitch actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
items.summary: Chase died from sepsis following a bout with meningitis in a Los Angeles hospital, her longtime manager told the BBC.

items.title: Trump says he will visit India as frosty relationship with Modi thaws
items.summary: Trump made the announcement during chat where he also vowed to protect India.

items.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
items.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

items.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
items.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

items.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
items.summary: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

items.title: First Russian shadow fleet tanker enters Channel since Smyrtos boarding
items.summary: Forwarder, a Russian-flagged ship which left port in Primorsk last week, entered the Channel on Wednesday evening.

items.title: 'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
items.summary: As the delivery vehicles increasing take to US streets, bans and protest groups are springing up.

items.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
items.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

items.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
items.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

items.title: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
items.summary: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

items.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
items.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

items.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
items.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

items.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles
items.summary: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

items.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
items.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

items.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
items.summary: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.
